name: CI test passes locally but fails ~30% in CI — flaky for 2 weeks
description: A specific test `test_order_aggregation` passes every time on local dev machines but fails about 30% of the time in CI (GitHub Actions on ubuntu-latest). The team has retried it 20+ times and been told 'it's flaky, just re-run'. It's now blocking merges.
task: A Python pytest test `test_order_aggregation` fails ~30% of the time in CI, always passes locally. Team has been dismissing it as 'flaky' for 2 weeks. It's now blocking merges. Symptom: `AssertionError: Expected 150.00, got 149.99`. The test creates orders, then calls an aggregation query. Walk through the systematic investigation: (1) your top 5 hypotheses ranked by likelihood, (2) how to reliably reproduce it in CI (not local), (3) how to distinguish each hypothesis, (4) the most likely root cause for a 'local passes / CI fails / AssertionError with near-miss value' pattern, (5) the fix. Do NOT accept 'it's flaky' as an answer — real bugs hide behind that.
expected_outcomes: Top hypotheses include: float precision, timezone, ordering, shared state, test isolation
Specific plan to reproduce in CI (binary-search the test suite, parallel workers, etc.)
Addresses that 'near-miss value' hints at float accumulation or ordering
Concrete fix for the most likely root cause
Rejects the 'just retry' habit with reasoning
